$comment: Plan 25 per-target struct variants — arm64-linux leg + the RED-ON-DISABLE pin. <sys/stat.h>'s `struct stat` (arch=arm64, format=elf) variant is the 128-byte glibc layout (DIVERGES from x86-64's 144), so `sizeof(struct stat)` folds to 128 → st_mode(40)+st_blocks(2)=42. Runs under qemu-aarch64 on the linux CI leg; cross-compiles locally. RED-ON-DISABLE: neuter the variant selector → arm64 gets the x86-64 144-byte variant → sizeof != 128 → exit 7. The release arm runs the optimizer over the struct shapes.
